records.chapter: Chapter on Liability for Those Who Take Payment for Repairing Something but Cause Damage Instead
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3918 - Ali bin Al-Hakam narrated from Isma'il bin Al-Sabbah, who said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a launderer to whom a garment is given, but he burns or tears it. Should he be held liable?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he is liable for the damage caused by his actions, for you gave it to him to repair or clean, not to ruin."
records.arabicText: 3918 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ اَلصَّبَّاحِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْقَصَّارِ يُسَلَّمُ إِلَيْهِ اَلْمَتَاعُ فَيُحْرِقُهُ أَوْ يَخْرِقُهُ أَ يُغَرَّمُهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ غُرِّمَهُ بِمَا جَنَتْ يَدُهُ فَإِنَّكَ إِنَّمَا أَعْطَيْتَهُ لِيُصْلِحَ وَ لَمْ تُعْطِهِ لِيُفْسِدَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3918 - Ali bin Al-Hakam narrated from Isma'il bin Al-Sabbah, who said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a launderer to whom a garment is given, but he burns or tears it. Should he be held liable?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, he is liable for the damage caused by his actions, for you gave it to him to repair or clean, not to ruin."

records.chapter: Chapter on Liability for Those Who Take Payment for Repairing Something but Cause Damage Instead
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3919 - Imam (as) said: "My father (as) used to hold the launderer and the goldsmith liable for any damage they caused. But, Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) would show leniency toward them."
records.arabicText: 3919 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُضَمِّنُ اَلْقَصَّارَ وَ اَلصَّوَّاغَ مَا أَفْسَدَا وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ يَتَفَضَّلُ عَلَيْهِمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3919 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) used to hold the launderer and the goldsmith liable for any damage they caused. But, Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) would show leniency toward them."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3920 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (as), regarding a camel driver who carries oil and claims that it was lost, spilled, or taken by robbers.
Imam (as) said: "If he brings trustworthy evidence that it was stolen, lost, or taken, then he is not liable. Otherwise, he is responsible for compensating it."
And regarding a man who transported food for another man in his boat, but the quantity decreased,
Imam (as) said: "He is liable."
I said to him: "Sometimes the quantity increases."
Imam (as) said: "Do you know that he added anything to it?"
I said: "No."
Imam (as) said: "Then it belongs to you."
records.arabicText: 3920 - رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي جَمَّالٍ يَحْمِلُ مَعَهُ اَلزَّيْتَ فَيَقُولُ قَدْ ذَهَبَ أَوْ أُهْرِقَ أَوْ قُطِعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلطَّرِيقُ "فَإِنْ جَاءَ عَلَيْهِ بِبَيِّنَةٍ عَادِلَةٍ أَنَّهُ قُطِعَ عَلَيْهِ أَوْ ذَهَبَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِلاَّ ضَمِنَ" وَ فِي رَجُلٍ حَمَلَ مَعَهُ رَجُلٌ فِي سَفِينَتِهِ طَعَاماً فَنَقَصَ قَالَ "هُوَ ضَامِنٌ" قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّهُ رُبَّمَا زَادَ قَالَ "تَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ زَادَ فِيهِ شَيْئاً" قُلْتُ لاَ قَالَ "هُوَ لَكَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3921 - Imam (as) said:
"Regarding the washer (ghassal) and the goldsmith (sawwagh), if something is stolen from them and they do not provide clear evidence proving that it was stolen, whether it is little or much, then they are liable. However, if they establish proof with clear evidence, then they are not liable. But if they fail to present evidence and merely claim that the item was lost, they are responsible for compensating it unless they provide proof to support their claim."
records.arabicText: 3921 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي اَلْغُسَّالِ وَ اَلصُّوَّاغِ مَا سُرِقَ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَلَمْ يَخْرُجْ بِبَيِّنَةٍ عَلَى أَمْرٍ بَيِّنٍ أَنَّهُ قَدْ سُرِقَ وَ كُلُّ قَلِيلٍ لَهُ أَوْ كَثِيرٍ فَإِنْ فَعَلَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُقِمْ بَيِّنَةً وَ زَعَمَ أَنَّهُ قَدْ ذَهَبَ اَلَّذِي اِدَّعَى فَقَدْ ضَمِنَهُ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ عَلَى قَوْلِهِ بَيِّنَةٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3921 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Regarding the washer (ghassal) and the goldsmith (sawwagh), if something is stolen from them and they do not provide clear evidence proving that it was stolen, whether it is little or much, then they are liable. However, if they establish proof with clear evidence, then they are not liable. But if they fail to present evidence and merely claim that the item was lost, they are responsible for compensating it unless they provide proof to support their claim."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3922 - Imam (as) said regarding a man who rents an animal to travel to a specified location, but the animal is lost:
"If he exceeded the agreed conditions, he is liable. If he entered a valley and did not secure the animal properly, he is liable. And if it fell into a well, he is liable because he did not take proper precautions to safeguard it."
records.arabicText: 3922 - وَ قَالَ : فِي رَجُلٍ تَكَارَى دَابَّةً إِلَى مَكَانٍ مَعْلُومٍ فَتَضِيعُ اَلدَّابَّةُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ جَازَ اَلشَّرْطَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ وَ إِنْ دَخَلَ وَادِياً فَلَمْ يُوثِقْهَا فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ وَ إِنْ سَقَطَتْ فِي بِئْرٍ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِأَنَّهُ لَمْ يَسْتَوْثِقْ مِنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3922 - Imam (as) said regarding a man who rents an animal to travel to a specified location, but the animal is lost:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he exceeded the agreed conditions, he is liable. If he entered a valley and did not secure the animal properly, he is liable. And if it fell into a well, he is liable because he did not take proper precautions to safeguard it."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3923 - It is narrated about a camel driver who was hired to transport oil to a certain land. He claimed that some of the oil skins were torn, causing the oil to spill.
Imam (as) said: "The owner may take the remaining oil if he wishes."
Imam (as) also said: "The camel driver's claim that the skins were torn is not accepted unless supported by trustworthy and just evidence."
And Imam (as) added: "Any man who rents an animal, and it is attacked by a wolf, which injures its eye and causes it to die, is liable for it unless he is a trustworthy and just person."
records.arabicText: 3923 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ جَمَّالٍ اُكْتُرِيَ مِنْهُ إِبِلٌ وَ بُعِثَ مَعَهُ بِزَيْتٍ إِلَى أَرْضٍ فَزَعَمَ أَنَّ بَعْضَ زِقَاقِ اَلزَّيْتِ اِنْخَرَقَ وَ أَهْرَاقَ اَلزَّيْتَ قَالَ "إِنَّهُ إِنْ شَاءَ أَخَذَ اَلزَّيْتَ" وَ قَالَ "اِنْخَرَقَ وَ لَكِنْ لاَ يُصَدَّقُ إِلاَّ بِبَيِّنَةٍ عَادِلَةٍ وَ أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ تَكَارَى دَابَّةً فَأَخَذَتْهَا اَلذِّئْبَةُ فَشَقَّتْ عَيْنَهَا فَنَفَقَتْ فَهُوَ لَهَا ضَامِنٌ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُسْلِماً عَدْلاً".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3924 - It is narrated from Ja'far bin Uthman, who said:
My father sent some goods to Syria with a camel driver, and the camel driver claimed that one of the loads was lost. I mentioned this to Abu Abdillah (as).
Imam (as) said: "Do you suspect him?"
I said: "No."
Imam (as) said: "Then do not hold him liable."
records.arabicText: 3924 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ : حَمَلَ أَبِي مَتَاعاً إِلَى اَلشَّامِ مَعَ جَمَّالٍ فَذَكَرَ أَنَّ حِمْلاً مِنْهُ ضَاعَ فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ "أَ تَتَّهِمُهُ" فَقُلْتُ لاَ قَالَ "فَلاَ تُضَمِّنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3924 - It is narrated from Ja'far bin Uthman, who said:
My father sent some goods to Syria with a camel driver, and the camel driver claimed that one of the loads was lost. I mentioned this to Abu Abdillah (as).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do you suspect him?"
I said: "No."
Imam (as) said: "Then do not hold him liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3925 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
I asked him about a launderer to whom I gave a garment, and he claimed that it was stolen from among his belongings.
Imam (as) said: "He must provide evidence that it was stolen from among his belongings, and if he does so, then he is not liable. Similarly, if it was stolen along with his belongings, he is not liable."
records.arabicText: 3925 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ قَصَّارٍ دَفَعْتُ إِلَيْهِ ثَوْباً فَزَعَمَ أَنَّهُ سُرِقَ مِنْ بَيْنِ ثِيَابِهِ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُقِيمَ اَلْبَيِّنَةَ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ سُرِقَ مِنْ بَيْنِ مَتَاعِهِ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِنْ سُرِقَ مَعَ مَتَاعِهِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3925 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
I asked him about a launderer to whom I gave a garment, and he claimed that it was stolen from among his belongings.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He must provide evidence that it was stolen from among his belongings, and if he does so, then he is not liable. Similarly, if it was stolen along with his belongings, he is not liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3926 - Uthman bin Ziyad narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
I said to Imam (as): "We had a camel driver who used to work for us. He loaded goods onto someone else's camel, and they were lost."
Imam (as) said: "Hold him liable and take compensation from him."
records.arabicText: 3926 - وَ رَوَى عُثْمَانُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ جَمَّالاً لَنَا كَانَ يُكَارِينَا فَحَمَلَ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ فَضَاعَ قَالَ "ضَمِّنْهُ وَ خُذْ مِنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3926 - Uthman bin Ziyad narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Imam (as): "We had a camel driver who used to work for us. He loaded goods onto someone else's camel, and they were lost."
Imam (as) said: "Hold him liable and take compensation from him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3927 - Commander of the Faithful (as) used to hold the dyer, launderer, and goldsmith liable as a precaution to protect people's belongings. However, he did not hold them liable for damages caused by drowning, fire, or overwhelming events.
And if a ship sinks along with its cargo, and people recover what washes up on the shore, it belongs to its owners, and they have the strongest claim to it. But whatever people dive for and the owner abandons, it belongs to those who recover it.
records.arabicText: 3927 - وَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُضَمِّنُ اَلصَّبَّاغَ وَ اَلْقَصَّارَ وَ اَلصَّائِغَ، اِحْتِيَاطاً عَلَى أَمْتِعَةِ اَلنَّاسِ وَ كَانَ لاَ يُضَمِّنُ مِنَ اَلْغَرَقِ وَ اَلْحَرَقِ وَ اَلشَّيْءِ اَلْغَالِبِ وَ إِذَا غَرِقَتِ اَلسَّفِينَةُ وَ مَا فِيهَا فَأَصَابَهُ اَلنَّاسُ فَمَا قَذَفَ بِهِ اَلْبَحْرُ عَلَى سَاحِلِهِ فَهُوَ لِأَهْلِهِ وَ هُمْ أَحَقُّ بِهِ وَ مَا غَاصَ عَلَيْهِ اَلنَّاسُ وَ تَرَكَهُ صَاحِبُهُ فَهُوَ لَهُمْ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3928 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
"The goldsmith, the launderer, and the weaver are not held liable unless they are suspected. In such cases, they must bring evidence, and they should be made to swear an oath and be warned, perhaps something may be revealed from them."
records.arabicText: 3928 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يُضَمَّنُ اَلصَّائِغُ وَ لاَ اَلْقَصَّارُ وَ لاَ اَلْحَائِكُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونُوا مُتَّهَمِينَ فَيَجِيئُونَ بِالْبَيِّنَةِ فَيُخَوَّفُ وَ يُسْتَحْلَفُ لَعَلَّهُ يُسْتَخْرَجُ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3928 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The goldsmith, the launderer, and the weaver are not held liable unless they are suspected. In such cases, they must bring evidence, and they should be made to swear an oath and be warned, perhaps something may be revealed from them."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3929 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) was presented with the case of a bathhouse attendant with whom clothes were deposited, but they were lost.
Imam (as) did not hold him liable and said: "He is merely a trustee."
records.arabicText: 3929 - وَ أُتِيَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِصَاحِبِ حَمَّامٍ وُضِعَتْ عِنْدَهُ اَلثِّيَابُ فَضَاعَتْ فَلَمْ يُضَمِّنْهُ وَ قَالَ "إِنَّمَا هُوَ أَمِينٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3929 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) was presented with the case of a bathhouse attendant with whom clothes were deposited, but they were lost.
Imam (as) did not hold him liable and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He is merely a trustee."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3930 - Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) held a Muslim man liable to pay the value of a pig that belonged to a Christian, which he had harmed.
records.arabicText: 3930 - وَ إِنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ضَمَّنَ رَجُلاً مُسْلِماً أَصَابَ خِنْزِيراً لِنَصْرَانِيٍّ قِيمَتَهُ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3931 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), regarding a man who hires a porter, and the porter either breaks what he is carrying or spills it.
Imam (as) said: "If he is trustworthy, then he is not liable. But if he is not trustworthy, then he is liable."
records.arabicText: 3931 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَسْتَأْجِرُ اَلْحَمَّالَ فَيَكْسِرُ اَلَّذِي يَحْمِلُ عَلَيْهِ أَوْ يُهَرِيقُهُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ مَأْمُوناً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ غَيْرَ مَأْمُونٍ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3931 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), regarding a man who hires a porter, and the porter either breaks what he is carrying or spills it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he is trustworthy, then he is not liable. But if he is not trustworthy, then he is liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3932 - Ibn Abi Nasr narrated from Dawood bin Sirhan, from Abu Abdillah (as):
"If a man carries goods on his head and accidentally strikes someone, causing their death or breaking something, then he is liable."
records.arabicText: 3932 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ سِرْحَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي رَجُلٍ حَمَلَ مَتَاعاً عَلَى رَأْسِهِ فَأَصَابَ إِنْسَاناً فَمَاتَ أَوْ اِنْكَسَرَ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3932 - Ibn Abi Nasr narrated from Dawood bin Sirhan, from Abu Abdillah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man carries goods on his head and accidentally strikes someone, causing their death or breaking something, then he is liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3933 - It is narrated from Muhammad bin Ali bin Mahbub, who said:
A man wrote to the 'Jurist' (Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as)) asking about a case where a person gave a garment to a launderer to clean, and the launderer then handed it over to another launderer to clean, but the garment was lost. Should the first launderer be required to compensate for what he entrusted to another?
Imam (as) wrote in reply: "He is liable for it unless the second launderer is trustworthy and reliable, God willing."
records.arabicText: 3933 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ قَالَ كَتَبَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى اَلْفَقِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ دَفَعَ ثَوْباً إِلَى اَلْقَصَّارِ لِيُقَصِّرَهُ فَدَفَعَهُ اَلْقَصَّارُ إِلَى قَصَّارٍ غَيْرِهِ لِيُقَصِّرَهُ فَضَاعَ اَلثَّوْبُ هَلْ يَجِبُ عَلَى اَلْقَصَّارِ أَنْ يَرُدَّ مَا دَفَعَهُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ إِنْ كَانَ اَلْقَصَّارُ مَأْمُوناً فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "هُوَ ضَامِنٌ لَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ ثِقَةً مَأْمُوناً إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3933 - It is narrated from Muhammad bin Ali bin Mahbub, who said:
A man wrote to the 'Jurist' (Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as)) asking about a case where a person gave a garment to a launderer to clean, and the launderer then handed it over to another launderer to clean, but the garment was lost. Should the first launderer be required to compensate for what he entrusted to another?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) wrote in reply: "He is liable for it unless the second launderer is trustworthy and reliable, God willing."

records.chapter: Chapter on Selling in the Shade
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith. - Hisham ibn Al-Hakam narrated:
"I used to sell Sabiri (a type of cloth) in the shade, and Abu Al-Hasan the First (as) passed by me while riding.
Imam (as) said to me: 'O Hisham, selling in the shade is deceit, and deceit is not permissible.'"
records.arabicText: 3980 - رُوِيَ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ اَلْحَكَمِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : كُنْتُ أَبِيعُ اَلسَّابِرِيَّ فِي اَلظِّلاَلِ فَمَرَّ بِي أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَاكِباً فَقَالَ لِي "يَا هِشَامُ إِنَّ اَلْبَيْعَ فِي اَلظِّلاَلِ غِشٌّ وَ اَلْغِشُّ لاَ يَحِلُّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith. - Hisham ibn Al-Hakam narrated:
"I used to sell Sabiri (a type of cloth) in the shade, and Abu Al-Hasan the First (as) passed by me while riding.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said to me: 'O Hisham, selling in the shade is deceit, and deceit is not permissible.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Barter and Transaction with Immediate Buyback (inah)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4032 - Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman narrated from more than one person, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who trades with another man over something.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm, as long as the origin of the item is lawful."
records.arabicText: 4032 - رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي الرَّجُلِ يُبَايِعُ الرَّجُلَ عَلَى الشَّيْ‌ءِ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ إِذَا كَانَ أَصْلُ الشَّيْ‌ءِ حَلَالًا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4032 - Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman narrated from more than one person, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who trades with another man over something.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm, as long as the origin of the item is lawful."

records.chapter: Chapter on Barter and Transaction with Immediate Buyback (inah)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4033 - Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated:
I said to Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as): "A man has wealth and goes to another man who sells him a pearl worth one hundred dirhams for one thousand dirhams, deferring the payment to a later time."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. My father (as) instructed me to do so, and I did it."
records.arabicText: 4033 - وَ- رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ‌ قُلْتُ لِلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ الرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ لَهُ الْمَالُ فَيَدْخُلُ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ يَبِيعُهُ لُؤْلُؤَةً تُسَاوِي مِائَةَ دِرْهَمٍ بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ يُؤَخِّرُ عَلَيْهِ الْمَالَ إِلَى وَقْتٍ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ قَدْ أَمَرَنِي أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَفَعَلْتُ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4033 - Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated:
I said to Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A man has wealth and goes to another man who sells him a pearl worth one hundred dirhams for one thousand dirhams, deferring the payment to a later time."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. My father (as) instructed me to do so, and I did it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Barter and Transaction with Immediate Buyback (inah)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4034 - It is narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I appointed a specific loan (aynah) for a man, and it became due. I said to him, 'Repay me.'
He said: 'I do not have it now, so specify another loan for me until I can repay you.'"
Imam (as) said: "Specify it for him until he repays you."
records.arabicText: 4034 - وَ- رُوِيَ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ الْجَمَّالِ‌ قَالَ‌ قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَيَّنْتُ رَجُلًا عِينَةً فَحَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ اقْضِنِي قَالَ لَيْسَ عِنْدِي فَعَيِّنِّي حَتَّى أَقْضِيَكَ قَالَ عَيِّنْهُ حَتَّى يَقْضِيَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4034 - It is narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I appointed a specific loan (aynah) for a man, and it became due. I said to him, 'Repay me.'
He said: 'I do not have it now, so specify another loan for me until I can repay you.'"
Imam (as) said: "Specify it for him until he repays you."

records.chapter: Chapter on Barter and Transaction with Immediate Buyback (inah)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4035 - It is narrated from Bakkar ibn Abi Bakr, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who has a debt owed to him by another man. When the debt becomes due, the debtor says to him, "Sell me some goods so that I may sell them and repay you what I owe."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4035 - وَ- رُوِيَ عَنْ بَكَّارِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الْمَالُ فَإِذَا حَلَّ قَالَ لَهُ بِعْنِي مَتَاعاً حَتَّى أَبِيعَهُ وَ أَقْضِيَكَ الَّذِي لَكَ عَلَيَّ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4035 - It is narrated from Bakkar ibn Abi Bakr, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who has a debt owed to him by another man. When the debt becomes due, the debtor says to him,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Sell me some goods so that I may sell them and repay you what I owe."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4036 - It is narrated from Ammar al-Sabati, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who sells dirhams for dinars on credit.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4036 - رُوِيَ عَنْ عَمَّارٍ السَّابَاطِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ‌ قُلْتُ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ‌ يَبِيعُ الدَّرَاهِمَ بِالدَّنَانِيرِ نَسِيئَةً قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ‌.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4036 - It is narrated from Ammar al-Sabati, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who sells dirhams for dinars on credit.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4037 - Hammad narrated from al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Silver for silver must be equal, and gold for gold must be equal, there should be neither an increase nor a delay. The one who gives extra and the one who demands extra will be in the Fire."
records.arabicText: 4037 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "اَلْفِضَّةُ بِالْفِضَّةِ مِثْلٌ بِمِثْلٍ وَ اَلذَّهَبُ بِالذَّهَبِ مِثْلٌ بِمِثْلٍ لَيْسَ فِيهِ زِيَادَةٌ وَ لاَ نَظِرَةٌ اَلزَّائِدُ وَ اَلْمُسْتَزِيدُ فِي اَلنَّارِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4037 - Hammad narrated from al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Silver for silver must be equal, and gold for gold must be equal, there should be neither an increase nor a delay. The one who gives extra and the one who demands extra will be in the Fire."

records.chapter: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4038 - Aban narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about a man who is owed dinars by another man, but he accepts dirhams instead. Then the price changes.
Imam (as) said: "It is based on the value at which he accepted them on that day. However, if he had accepted dinars and not dirhams, then his dinars remain owed to him, and he may take them in their original amount whenever he wishes."
records.arabicText: 4038 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ اَلدَّنَانِيرُ فَيَأْخُذُ مِنْهُ دَرَاهِمَ ثُمَّ يَتَغَيَّرُ اَلسِّعْرُ قَالَ "هِيَ لَهُ عَلَى اَلسِّعْرِ اَلَّذِي أَخَذَهَا يَوْمَئِذٍ وَ إِنْ أَخَذَ دَنَانِيرَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ دَرَاهِمُ عِنْدَهُ فَدَنَانِيرُهُ عَلَيْهِ يَأْخُذُهَا بِرُءُوسِهَا مَتَى شَاءَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4038 - Aban narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about a man who is owed dinars by another man, but he accepts dirhams instead. Then the price changes.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "It is based on the value at which he accepted them on that day. However, if he had accepted dinars and not dirhams, then his dinars remain owed to him, and he may take them in their original amount whenever he wishes."

records.chapter: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4039 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A man comes to me with dirhams, and I buy them from him with dinars. Then I give him a pouch containing more dinars than the value of his dirhams.
I say to him: 'From these dinars, such and such amount is the price of your dirhams.'
He takes the pouch from me, then returns it and says: 'Keep it with you for me.'"
Imam (as) said: "If the pouch contains enough to cover the value of his dirhams, then there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4039 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ حَنَانِ بْنِ سَدِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّهُ يَأْتِينِي اَلرَّجُلُ وَ مَعَهُ اَلدَّرَاهِمُ فَأَشْتَرِيهَا مِنْهُ بِالدَّنَانِيرِ ثُمَّ أُعْطِيهِ كِيساً فِيهِ دَنَانِيرُ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ دَرَاهِمِهِ فَأَقُولُ لَكَ مِنْ هَذِهِ اَلدَّنَانِيرِ كَذَا وَ كَذَا دِينَاراً ثَمَنُ دَرَاهِمِكَ فَيَقْبِضُ اَلْكِيسَ مِنِّي ثُمَّ يَرُدُّهُ عَلَيَّ وَ يَقُولُ أَثْبِتْهَا لِي عِنْدَكَ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ فِي اَلْكِيسِ وَفَاءٌ بِثَمَنِ دَرَاهِمِهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4039 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A man comes to me with dirhams, and I buy them from him with dinars. Then I give him a pouch containing more dinars than the value of his dirhams.
I say to him: 'From these dinars, such and such amount is the price of your dirhams.'
He takes the pouch from me, then returns it and says: 'Keep it with you for me.'"
Imam (as) said: "If the pouch contains enough to cover the value of his dirhams, then there is no harm in it."
